title: Ari Shapiro: 'How to Fix Capitalism'
summary: NPR host and bestselling author Ari Shapiro devotes his entire Substack newsletter to Incorruptible, calling it the most exciting book he's read in years. Shapiro walks through Ries's central argument — that companies like Costco, Patagonia, and Duolingo aren't just good corporate citizens, they actually outperform their competitors — and contrasts it with cautionary tales of Boeing, Whole Foods, and Polaroid. What makes Shapiro's review distinctive is his framing of Ries's argument as 'the call coming from inside the house' — unlike distant policy proposals, this is a founder and CEO explaining how to fix capitalism from within.
blockquote: Reading it made me so hopeful and excited that I've been telling everyone I know about it. Unlike other solutions that feel distant and slightly unrealistic, this is the call coming from inside the house.
